text.title: JVM One-Tag Release via JReleaser Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: A push of a SemVer tag (vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) MUST be the sole release trigger; ad-hoc local `mvn deploy` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: One trigger → reproducible artefacts. Local deploys produce unsigned / unauditable builds.

rule.statement: jreleaser.yml MUST be committed with distribution / signing / changelog config; secrets via env, never in YAML.
rule.rationale: Committed config makes the release reproducible; secrets in YAML leak to source.

rule.statement: Changelog generation depends on Conventional Commits; repo MUST enforce them (commitlint / pre-commit).
rule.rationale: Without conventional commits, JReleaser changelog is noise.

rule.statement: All published artefacts MUST be GPG-signed; signing key in CI secret store.
rule.rationale: Maven Central rejects unsigned artefacts; downstream consumers expect signatures.

rule.statement: JReleaser MUST create the GitHub release with changelog + artefact uploads in the same run as the Maven Central staging.
rule.rationale: Two sources of release truth diverge; one orchestrator avoids it.

rule.statement: If repo doesn't publish externally OR team won't adopt conventional commits OR JVM is just one slice of a polyglot release, STOP.
rule.rationale: JReleaser's value is JVM + central registry; outside that, cost > value.
